context: Tests whether the agent correctly distinguishes pull-request activity types from draft state and orders pnpm setup before setup-node's pnpm cache integration.

checklist.name: Draft behavior explained
checklist.description: The explanation states that pull_request activity types such as opened and synchronize can fire for draft PRs, so including ready_for_review does not itself suppress draft CI

checklist.name: Draft-aware job gate
checklist.description: Each pull-request entry job, or a shared root job that gates every dependent verification job, has an `if:` condition requiring `github.event.pull_request.draft == false` while remaining valid for every configured event

checklist.name: Ready event retained
checklist.description: The pull_request trigger retains ready_for_review so verification starts when a draft is marked ready

checklist.name: Update events retained
checklist.description: The trigger still handles opened, synchronize, and reopened pull-request activity

checklist.name: pnpm installed first
checklist.description: pnpm/action-setup runs before actions/setup-node when setup-node is configured with `cache: pnpm`

checklist.name: pnpm cache retained
checklist.description: actions/setup-node still configures the Node version and enables the pnpm store cache after pnpm is available

checklist.name: Frozen install
checklist.description: The workflow installs dependencies with pnpm using the frozen lockfile behavior explicitly or through pnpm's documented CI default

checklist.name: Unprivileged PR event
checklist.description: The workflow keeps `pull_request` and does not switch to `pull_request_target` or add write permissions or secrets

checklist.name: Minimal correction
checklist.description: The solution changes only the draft gate and pnpm bootstrap order without unrelated workflow redesign
